title: Architecture, or implementation detail?
intro: The line that trips up most engineers: which decisions shape the architecture, and which are things you defer?

buckets.label: Implementation detail
buckets.hint: How a black box is built inside

cards.text: Split the system into a payments service and an orders service
Services communicate over async message queues, not direct calls
The API is stateless so it can scale horizontally
Data is partitioned by customer region across three databases
The orders service is written in Go rather than Java
Use React instead of Vue for the admin dashboard
Sort the results with quicksort inside one handler

debrief: Architecture is about **structure and communication**: service boundaries, protocols, statelessness, data distribution — the shape of the black boxes and how they talk. Language, framework, an internal algorithm, or a class name live *inside* a black box and can change without touching the architecture. Decide the topology first; defer the details.
